_provenance.how: Copied from the ACTUAL `mcp__minsky__session_pr_merge` tool returns observed in conversation f00dfb7d-17e6-42ce-b0d9-00716e2fa10b, which merged PR #2227 (mt#3066) and PR #2232 (mt#3101). Values are the real ids from those merges.
_provenance.why: These fixtures exist because the previous ones did NOT. mt#3066's verification script authored its own `ToolHookInput` with `tool_result.session.sessionId` — a shape invented to match the resolver rather than captured from a real call. It passed while production skipped every merge. A hand-written fixture tests the reader against itself; only a captured one tests the reader against reality. Do not edit these to make a test pass — re-capture them.
_provenance.recapture: Call `session_pr_merge`, copy its verbatim return into `toolResult`, and record the `tool_input` you passed.

taskInvoked._description: The COMMON form: merge invoked with `task:` and no `sessionId`. This is the shape that never resolved — the id is a string at tool_result.result.session.

sessionIdInvoked._description: The same tool invoked with an explicit `sessionId`. This form always resolved, via tool_input — which is why the defect was invisible whenever a merge happened to be called this way.
